nodes.question: What brings you to the AI Front Door today?

options.label: I want to create or propose a new AI use case
I have an idea and need help shaping it
I want to understand what AI projects are active
I need to report a concern or incident

nodes.question: Why is AI the right approach for this task?
nodes.helpText: We ask this to ensure AI is being used where it genuinely adds value — not just because it's available. The manual calls this the 'Why AI?' assessment.

options.label: There's a clear problem and AI outperforms manual or conventional approaches
We're exploring whether AI could add value
Not sure — someone suggested using AI

nodes.question: What kind of information will this use case handle?
nodes.helpText: This determines the data governance and security controls required. Think about both the inputs the AI will receive and the outputs it will produce.

options.label: Public information or ordinary internal data
Controlled internal information with limited sensitivity
Personal data, special category, classified, export-controlled, customer-restricted, or legally privileged

nodes.question: How much influence will the AI have on decisions or outcomes?
nodes.helpText: Consider the worst case — not just the intended use, but what happens if someone treats AI output as definitive.

options.label: Drafting, research, or admin support only
Supports internal decisions but does not decide or act
Materially shapes or determines operational, customer, workforce, safety, security, legal, or financial outcomes

nodes.question: Who will use or rely on the AI's outputs?
nodes.helpText: External reliance changes the approval pathway significantly. If customers or external parties will see or depend on the output, that triggers additional controls.

options.label: Internal working aid — only I or my immediate team will use it
Internal operational teams rely on it for their work
Customer-facing, externally relied upon, or embedded in a deliverable

nodes.question: What can the AI system actually do?
nodes.helpText: This assesses the autonomy level. A system that can take actions — sending emails, modifying records, triggering workflows — needs stronger controls than one that only drafts content for human review.
nodes.manualReference: Section 2.2 — Autonomy and Action Authority; Section 3.4

options.label: Generates content, suggestions, or analysis for human review before any action
Can recommend or trigger bounded steps, but with human checkpoints
Can act on systems, send communications, change or delete data, or self-initiate actions

nodes.question: Does this involve any specialist or regulated domain?
nodes.helpText: Certain domains require additional specialist review regardless of other factors. If you're unsure whether your domain qualifies, select the middle option and AIDEX will advise.

options.label: No — standard business domain with no special regulatory or safety considerations
Operationally relevant but not in a restricted or regulated domain
Safety-critical, defence, security-sensitive, regulated, workforce decisioning, or mission-relevant
